sonnet_review.biasedText: This simple fact defies the president's opponents who have since spread lies and falsehoods about his relationship with Epstein and convicted associate Maxwell.
sonnet_review.reason: The phrasing uses loaded language (“simple fact,” “lies and falsehoods”) to characterize critics and assert conclusiveness. While I did not flag it originally, this is a defensible identification of biased wording in the author’s voice.

Labeling Trump a “whistleblower” is a positive, interpretive framing not strictly required by the facts presented. I did not flag it, but it is a reasonable call-out of loaded terminology used by the author, not as a quote.

sonnet_review.biasedText: Trump has consistently denied any wrongdoing in relation to Epstein, with whom he says he severed contact decades ago, and has not been accused of any crimes by Epstein's victims.
sonnet_review.reason: The sentence presents exculpatory framing without balancing context about prior associations. I did not flag this, but the slant argument is defensible as a matter of emphasis and story structure.

gpt_review.biasedText: This simple fact defies the president’s opponents who have since spread lies and falsehoods about his relationship with Epstein and convicted associate Maxwell.
gpt_review.reason: The language portrays critics as deliberate liars and asserts finality (“simple fact”), which reflects partisan framing. I did not flag it, but this is a reasonable, text-grounded bias detection.
